text:
Once upon a time, in a bustling city filled with towering structures and endless traffic, there lived a curious rabbit named Hoppy. Hoppy was small and furry, with an insatiable appetite for adventure. Every morning, before the sun peeked over the rooftops, Hoppy would leap out from his burrow, dressed in his favorite pastel green outfit, into the busy street.

One day, during an especially busy afternoon, Hoppy spotted something peculiar. A small, colorful band was performing in a park. Everyone was clapping and dancing. Hoppy's ears stood upright; he had never seen anyone dancing like that before. It was as if the music awakened their inner springs!

Driven by curiosity, Hoppy joined the group. The conductor, an elderly owl with a twinkling eye, invited him to play the tambourine. With all the enthusiasm a rabbit could muster, Hoppy began to beat time. Soon, people of all ages were joining in, their movements synchronized
